qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Denial of service via unbounded package downloads and resource allocation
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding concerns unbounded downloads, thread pools, and dependency solver resource use, not Poetry wheel entry path handling in WheelDestination.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Path traversal in archive extraction allows arbitrary file write
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding targets helpers.extractall() and sdist/archive extraction, while the advisory targets wheel_installer.py WheelDestination path construction for wheel member names.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Arbitrary code execution via plugin loading without sandboxing
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding concerns plugin entry point loading in plugin_manager.py, not unsafe wheel member path joins in wheel_installer.py.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: SSRF via missing URL validation in package repository responses
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding concerns repository URL construction with urljoin(), not wheel installation path traversal through WheelDestination.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No final qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described root cause: Poetry's wheel installer builds destination paths by directly joining untrusted wheel entry names in WheelDestination without resolving and checking containment. The closest finding is another path traversal, but it is rooted in helpers.extractall() archive extraction and sdist/inspection paths rather than wheel_installer.py.
evidence: Advisory GHSA-2599-h6xx-hpxp names src/poetry/installation/wheel_installer.py and WheelDestination path construction from crafted wheel entries as the vulnerable code/root cause.

path_traversal_archive_extraction/vulnerability_report.json names src/poetry/utils/helpers.py extractall(), src/poetry/installation/chef.py, and src/poetry/inspection/info.py as the vulnerable path and states that wheel installation has separate WheelDestination handling.
plugin_code_execution, ssrf_url_validation, and dos_unbounded_downloads target plugin loading, repository URL construction, and resource exhaustion respectively, not wheel member path containment.
blockers_to_higher_tier: No advisory-matched qualifying finding exists, so Tier1, Tier2, and Tier3 are not applicable.
Per instruction, no PoC was executed because PoCs are only run for advisory-matched qualifying findings.
